reading-time.name: Contract Reading Time Calculator
reading-time.description: Paste your contract text and get an estimated reading time based on word count and legal complexity. Helps you plan how long it will take to review a document.
reading-time.shortDescription: Estimate how long it takes to read a contract.

faqs.question: How is reading time calculated?
faqs.answer: We use an average reading speed of 200 words per minute for legal text, which is slower than regular text due to complexity.

faqs.question: Does complexity affect the estimate?
faqs.answer: Yes, legal jargon density adds extra time to account for re-reading and comprehension.

faqs.question: Is this accurate for all contracts?
faqs.answer: It's an estimate. Complex financial or technical contracts may take longer.

word-counter.name: Contract Word Counter
word-counter.description: Get detailed statistics about your contract including word count, character count, sentence count, paragraph count, and estimated pages.
word-counter.shortDescription: Get detailed word and character statistics.

faqs.question: How are pages estimated?
faqs.answer: We estimate 250 words per page for standard legal documents.

faqs.question: Does it count headers and footers?
faqs.answer: It counts all text you paste into the tool.

faqs.question: Can I use this for non-legal documents?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The word counter works for any text.

jargon-translator.name: Legal Jargon Translator
jargon-translator.description: Look up common legal terms and get plain-English explanations. Search our dictionary of 100+ legal terms to understand what your contract actually says.
jargon-translator.shortDescription: Translate legal terms to plain English.

faqs.question: How many terms are in the dictionary?
faqs.answer: We currently have 100+ common legal terms with plain-English definitions.

faqs.question: Is this legal advice?
faqs.answer: No. This tool provides general explanations. Always consult a lawyer for specific legal questions.

faqs.question: Can I suggest new terms?
faqs.answer: We're always expanding our dictionary. Contact us with suggestions.

clause-finder.name: Contract Clause Finder
clause-finder.description: Paste your contract and search for common clause types like indemnification, termination, non-compete, confidentiality, and more. Quickly find the sections that matter most.
clause-finder.shortDescription: Find specific clauses in your contracts.

faqs.question: What clause types can it find?
faqs.answer: It searches for 15+ common clause types including indemnification, termination, non-compete, confidentiality, liability, and more.

faqs.question: How accurate is the finder?
faqs.answer: It uses keyword matching to identify likely clause locations. Review the context to confirm.

faqs.question: Does it work with any contract format?
faqs.answer: Yes, as long as you paste the text content of your contract.

cancellation-deadline.name: Cancellation Deadline Calculator
cancellation-deadline.description: Calculate your cancellation deadline based on your signup date and cooling-off period. Know exactly when your right to cancel expires.
cancellation-deadline.shortDescription: Calculate when your cancellation window closes.

faqs.question: What is a cooling-off period?
faqs.answer: A cooling-off period is a time window after signing a contract during which you can cancel without penalty.

faqs.question: Are cooling-off periods the same everywhere?
faqs.answer: No, they vary by jurisdiction and contract type. Common periods are 3, 7, 14, and 30 days.

faqs.question: Does this include weekends?
faqs.answer: You can choose whether to count business days only or calendar days.

notice-period.name: Notice Period Calculator
notice-period.description: Calculate your notice period end date based on your start date and notice terms. Works for employment, rental, and service agreements.
notice-period.shortDescription: Calculate when your notice period ends.

faqs.question: What notice periods are common?
faqs.answer: Common periods include 2 weeks, 30 days, 60 days, and 90 days depending on the agreement type.

faqs.question: Does it account for weekends?
faqs.answer: You can toggle between calendar days and business days.

faqs.question: Can I use this for rental agreements?
faqs.answer: Yes! It works for any agreement with a notice period.

late-fee.name: Late Fee Calculator
late-fee.description: Calculate the total late fee on an overdue payment. Enter the principal amount, fee rate, and number of days late to see what you owe or are owed.
late-fee.shortDescription: Calculate late fees on overdue payments.

faqs.question: How are late fees typically calculated?
faqs.answer: Most late fees are either a flat amount or a percentage of the outstanding balance, charged daily or monthly.

faqs.question: Are there legal limits on late fees?
faqs.answer: Yes, many jurisdictions cap late fees. Check your local laws.

faqs.question: Can I use this for invoices?
faqs.answer: Yes, it works for any type of overdue payment.

refund-checker.name: Refund Eligibility Checker
refund-checker.description: Answer a few simple questions about your purchase and situation to check if you might be eligible for a refund. Covers common consumer scenarios.
refund-checker.shortDescription: Check if you qualify for a refund.

faqs.question: Is the result legally binding?
faqs.answer: No. This provides general guidance based on common consumer protection rules. Always check your specific contract and local laws.

faqs.question: What scenarios does it cover?
faqs.answer: It covers online purchases, in-store purchases, services, subscriptions, and defective products.

faqs.question: Does it vary by country?
faqs.answer: The checker provides general guidance. Specific rights vary by jurisdiction.

non-compete.name: Non-Compete Duration Checker
non-compete.description: Enter your non-compete clause duration and state to see general enforceability information. Some states restrict or ban non-competes entirely.

faqs.question: Are non-competes enforceable?
faqs.answer: It varies widely by state. Some states like California generally don't enforce them.

faqs.question: What's a typical non-compete duration?
faqs.answer: Most courts consider 1-2 years reasonable. Longer periods are harder to enforce.

faqs.question: Should I sign a non-compete?
faqs.answer: Consult an employment lawyer before signing. This tool provides general info only.

freelance-rate.name: Freelance Rate Calculator
freelance-rate.description: Convert between hourly rates, project fees, and annual income. Factor in taxes, expenses, and billable hours to find your true freelance rate.
freelance-rate.shortDescription: Convert between hourly, project, and annual rates.

faqs.question: How do I calculate my hourly rate?
faqs.answer: Start with your desired annual income, add taxes and expenses, then divide by your estimated billable hours.

faqs.question: What are typical billable hours?
faqs.answer: Most freelancers bill 1,000-1,500 hours per year out of 2,080 total working hours.

faqs.question: Should I include benefits costs?
faqs.answer: Yes! Factor in health insurance, retirement, and other benefits you'd get as an employee.

invoice-interest.name: Invoice Late Interest Calculator
invoice-interest.description: Calculate the interest owed on late invoice payments. Enter the invoice amount, interest rate, and days overdue to determine the total amount due.

faqs.question: What interest rate should I charge?
faqs.answer: Common rates are 1-1.5% per month. Check your contract terms and local regulations.

faqs.question: Can I legally charge interest on late payments?
faqs.answer: In most cases, yes, if it's specified in your contract or invoice terms.

faqs.question: How is daily interest calculated?
faqs.answer: Annual rate divided by 365, multiplied by the number of days overdue and the principal amount.

nda-generator.name: NDA Template Generator
nda-generator.description: Generate a basic Non-Disclosure Agreement by filling out a simple form. Customize parties, duration, scope, and jurisdiction.
nda-generator.shortDescription: Generate a basic NDA from a form.

faqs.question: Is this NDA legally binding?
faqs.answer: This generates a template. Have it reviewed by a lawyer before using it for important agreements.

faqs.question: What type of NDA is generated?
faqs.answer: A mutual or one-way NDA depending on your selection, covering standard confidentiality terms.

faqs.question: Can I customize the template?
faqs.answer: Yes, the generated text can be copied and edited as needed.

privacy-generator.name: Privacy Policy Generator
privacy-generator.description: Generate a privacy policy for your website or app. Answer questions about your data practices and get a formatted policy document.
privacy-generator.shortDescription: Create a privacy policy for your website.

faqs.question: Does this comply with GDPR?
faqs.answer: It includes common GDPR provisions but should be reviewed by a legal professional for full compliance.

faqs.question: Can I use this for my app?
faqs.answer: Yes, the generator covers both websites and mobile applications.

faqs.question: How often should I update my privacy policy?
faqs.answer: Update whenever your data practices change, or at least annually.

complaint-generator.name: Complaint Letter Generator
complaint-generator.description: Generate a formal complaint letter. Fill in the details about your issue and get a professionally formatted letter ready to send.

faqs.question: What types of complaints does this cover?
faqs.answer: Product issues, service complaints, billing disputes, and general consumer complaints.

faqs.question: Should I send the letter by mail or email?
faqs.answer: For formal complaints, certified mail provides proof of delivery. Email is faster for initial contact.

faqs.question: What should I include with my letter?
faqs.answer: Include copies of receipts, contracts, photos, or any evidence supporting your complaint.

tos-generator.name: Terms of Service Generator
tos-generator.description: Generate terms of service for your website or application. Covers user agreements, liability limitations, and standard legal provisions.
tos-generator.shortDescription: Create terms of service for your site.

faqs.question: Do I legally need terms of service?
faqs.answer: While not always legally required, they protect your business and set user expectations.

faqs.question: What's the difference between ToS and a privacy policy?
faqs.answer: ToS governs user behavior and your liability. Privacy policy covers data collection and use.

faqs.question: Can users agree by using my site?
faqs.answer: Browsewrap agreements are weaker than clickwrap. Consider requiring explicit agreement.

red-flag-scanner.name: Contract Red Flag Scanner
red-flag-scanner.description: Paste your contract and let AI identify risky clauses, unfair terms, and potential legal issues with severity ratings and recommendations.
red-flag-scanner.shortDescription: AI scans contracts for risky clauses.

faqs.question: How does the scanner work?
faqs.answer: It uses AI to analyze your contract text and identify clauses that could be problematic, one-sided, or contain hidden risks.

faqs.question: Is this legal advice?
faqs.answer: No. This is an AI-powered analysis tool for educational purposes. Always consult a lawyer for legal decisions.

faqs.question: How long does analysis take?
faqs.answer: Typically 5-15 seconds depending on the length of the contract.

nda-fairness.name: NDA Fairness Score
nda-fairness.description: Paste an NDA and get a fairness score from 0-100, with a breakdown of strengths, concerns, and recommendations for negotiation.

faqs.question: What does the score mean?
faqs.answer: A score of 70+ indicates a generally fair NDA. Below 40 suggests significant one-sided terms that should be negotiated.

faqs.question: Does it work for mutual NDAs?
faqs.answer: Yes, it analyzes both mutual and one-way NDAs and considers whether the obligations are balanced.

faqs.question: Can I use this before signing?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — that's the ideal use case. Review the score and concerns before signing any NDA.

lease-analyzer.name: Lease Agreement Analyzer
lease-analyzer.description: Paste your lease agreement and get a summary of key terms, hidden risks, and recommendations. Perfect for renters reviewing a new lease.

faqs.question: What types of leases does it analyze?
faqs.answer: Residential and commercial leases. It identifies key terms like rent, deposit, duration, and maintenance obligations.

faqs.question: Will it catch hidden fees?
faqs.answer: The AI looks for unusual charges, escalation clauses, and fees that may not be obvious at first reading.

faqs.question: Should I trust the analysis completely?
faqs.answer: Use it as a starting point. For significant lease commitments, always have a lawyer review the full document.

terms-summarizer.name: Terms & Conditions Summarizer
terms-summarizer.description: Paste any Terms & Conditions or legal document and get a plain-English summary organized by topic, with concerning sections highlighted.

faqs.question: How long can the document be?
faqs.answer: It works best with documents under 10,000 words. For longer documents, paste the most relevant sections.

faqs.question: What does 'Concern' mean?
faqs.answer: Sections flagged as concerns contain terms that are unusual, potentially unfavorable, or worth paying extra attention to.

faqs.question: Can I use this for privacy policies?
faqs.answer: Yes! It works for any legal document — T&C, privacy policies, EULAs, and more.

contract-comparison.name: Contract Comparison Tool
contract-comparison.description: Paste two contracts side by side and get an AI-powered comparison highlighting key differences, changed terms, and which version favors which party.

faqs.question: What should I compare?
faqs.answer: Compare different versions of the same contract, competing offers, or a proposed contract against a standard template.

faqs.question: Does it show exact text differences?
faqs.answer: It highlights semantic differences — changes in meaning, scope, and obligations — rather than word-by-word diffs.

faqs.question: Can I compare different types of contracts?
faqs.answer: You can, but the most useful comparisons are between similar contract types (e.g., two employment contracts).

clause-explainer.name: Clause Explainer
clause-explainer.description: Paste any legal clause and get a plain-English explanation, risk assessment, practical implications, and negotiation tips.

faqs.question: What kind of clauses can I explain?
faqs.answer: Any legal clause from any type of contract — employment, lease, NDA, terms of service, or any other agreement.

faqs.question: Is the explanation legally accurate?
faqs.answer: The AI provides general interpretations. For clauses with significant financial or legal implications, consult a lawyer.

faqs.question: Can I paste multiple clauses?
faqs.answer: For best results, paste one clause at a time. This ensures focused, detailed explanations.

contract-expiration.name: Contract Expiration Tracker
contract-expiration.description: Enter your contract dates and track expirations in one place. See which contracts are expiring soon, active, or already expired.

faqs.question: Can I track multiple contracts?
faqs.answer: Yes, add as many contracts as you need and see them sorted by expiration date.

faqs.question: Does it send notifications?
faqs.answer: This is a manual tracker. Check back periodically to review your upcoming expirations.

faqs.question: What counts as 'expiring soon'?
faqs.answer: Contracts expiring within 30 days are flagged as expiring soon.

signature-block.name: Signature Block Generator
signature-block.description: Generate properly formatted signature blocks for individuals, corporations, LLCs, and partnerships. Copy and paste into your contracts.

faqs.question: What entity types are supported?
faqs.answer: Individual, corporation, LLC, and partnership signature blocks.

faqs.question: Is the format legally standard?
faqs.answer: Yes, the generated blocks follow common legal formatting conventions.

faqs.question: Can I use this for any contract?
faqs.answer: Yes, signature blocks are standard across all contract types.

contract-checklist.name: Contract Checklist Generator
contract-checklist.description: Select your contract type and get a comprehensive review checklist. Track which items you've reviewed with an interactive progress bar.

faqs.question: What contract types are covered?
faqs.answer: Employment contracts, NDAs, lease agreements, and service agreements.

faqs.question: Can I save my progress?
faqs.answer: Currently the checklist resets when you leave the page. Copy the items for your records.

faqs.question: Is this a substitute for legal review?
faqs.answer: No, use it as a starting point to identify key items before consulting a lawyer.

amendment-drafter.name: Contract Amendment Drafter
amendment-drafter.description: Generate a basic contract amendment by filling in the original contract details and describing the changes. Creates a formatted amendment document.
amendment-drafter.shortDescription: Draft a contract amendment from a form.

faqs.question: Is this amendment legally binding?
faqs.answer: This generates a template. Have it reviewed by a lawyer before using it.

faqs.question: Can I amend any type of contract?
faqs.answer: Yes, the amendment format is standard and works for most contract types.

faqs.question: What if I need multiple amendments?
faqs.answer: Generate a new amendment for each set of changes, incrementing the amendment number.

contract-value.name: Contract Value Calculator
contract-value.description: Calculate the total value of a contract including renewals and annual escalation rates. See initial term value, renewal value, and monthly averages.
contract-value.shortDescription: Calculate total contract value with renewals.

faqs.question: What is an escalation rate?
faqs.answer: An annual percentage increase applied to payments, commonly 2-5% per year.

faqs.question: Does it handle different payment frequencies?
faqs.answer: Yes — weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual payment schedules.

faqs.question: How are renewals calculated?
faqs.answer: Each renewal period uses the same duration as the initial term, with escalation continuing.

warranty-tracker.name: Warranty Expiration Calculator
warranty-tracker.description: Track warranty expiration dates for your purchases. Enter product details and warranty length to see which warranties are still active.
warranty-tracker.shortDescription: Track warranty end dates for purchases.

faqs.question: Can I track multiple products?
faqs.answer: Yes, add as many products as you need.

faqs.question: What warranty units are supported?
faqs.answer: Days, months, and years.

faqs.question: Does it work for extended warranties?
faqs.answer: Yes, just enter the total warranty period including any extensions.

statute-of-limitations.name: Statute of Limitations Lookup
statute-of-limitations.description: Look up the statute of limitations for common legal claims by state. Find out how long you have to file for contract disputes, personal injury, fraud, and more.
statute-of-limitations.shortDescription: Look up filing deadlines by state.

faqs.question: What claim types are covered?
faqs.answer: Written contracts, oral contracts, personal injury, property damage, and fraud.

faqs.question: Are these exact deadlines?
faqs.answer: These are general guidelines. Discovery rules and tolling can extend deadlines. Consult a lawyer.

faqs.question: Do all states have the same limits?
faqs.answer: No, statute of limitations varies significantly by state and claim type.

small-claims-limit.name: Small Claims Court Limit Checker
small-claims-limit.description: Check the maximum dollar amount you can sue for in small claims court in your state. Enter your claim amount to see if it qualifies.
small-claims-limit.shortDescription: Check small claims $ limits by state.

faqs.question: What is small claims court?
faqs.answer: A simplified court for resolving disputes involving smaller amounts of money, without needing a lawyer.

faqs.question: Can I file above the limit?
faqs.answer: You can reduce your claim to fit the limit (waiving the excess) or file in a higher court.

faqs.question: Are these limits current?
faqs.answer: We update limits regularly, but verify with your local court as limits change.

consumer-rights-quiz.name: Consumer Rights Quiz
consumer-rights-quiz.description: Test your knowledge of consumer protection rights with our interactive quiz. Learn about return policies, warranties, billing disputes, and more.

faqs.question: How many questions are there?
faqs.answer: The quiz has 8 questions covering various consumer rights topics.

faqs.question: Is this based on US law?
faqs.answer: Yes, the questions are primarily based on US federal and state consumer protection laws.

faqs.question: Can I retake the quiz?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can restart the quiz as many times as you like.

dispute-letter.name: Dispute Letter Generator
dispute-letter.description: Generate a formal dispute letter for billing errors, credit report inaccuracies, service complaints, or product issues. Includes relevant legal references.

faqs.question: What types of disputes are supported?
faqs.answer: Billing disputes, credit report disputes, service disputes, and product disputes.

faqs.question: Should I send this by mail or email?
faqs.answer: For billing and credit disputes, certified mail provides proof of delivery and triggers legal protections.

faqs.question: Is this letter legally sufficient?
faqs.answer: It follows standard dispute letter formats. Customize it to your situation for best results.

salary-converter.name: Salary to Hourly Converter
salary-converter.description: Convert between annual salary and hourly rate. Factor in benefits overhead, hours per week, and weeks per year to see the true cost of employment.
salary-converter.shortDescription: Convert salary to hourly rate and back.

faqs.question: What is benefits overhead?
faqs.answer: The additional cost of employing someone beyond salary — typically 25-40% for health insurance, retirement, taxes, etc.

faqs.question: Why use 52 weeks?
faqs.answer: 52 weeks is standard for a full year. Reduce for unpaid time off.

faqs.question: Does this include taxes?
faqs.answer: The benefits overhead percentage can include employer-paid taxes like FICA.

pto-calculator.name: PTO Calculator
pto-calculator.description: Calculate your accrued paid time off based on your accrual rate, employment tenure, and days already used. See remaining PTO in days and hours.

faqs.question: What's a typical accrual rate?
faqs.answer: Common rates are 1-2 days per month, or 4-6 hours per pay period.

faqs.question: What is a PTO cap?
faqs.answer: Many companies cap the total PTO you can accumulate. Once you hit the cap, you stop accruing until you use some.

faqs.question: Are sick days included?
faqs.answer: It depends on your company. Some have separate sick leave; others combine all time off into PTO.

wrongful-termination.name: Wrongful Termination Checklist
wrongful-termination.description: Answer questions about your termination and get an assessment of potential wrongful termination indicators, including discrimination and retaliation flags.

faqs.question: Is this legal advice?
faqs.answer: No. This provides general information to help you decide if consulting an employment lawyer is warranted.

faqs.question: What counts as wrongful termination?
faqs.answer: Termination based on discrimination, retaliation, breach of contract, or violation of public policy.

faqs.question: Should I consult a lawyer?
faqs.answer: If you see multiple red flags, consulting an employment lawyer is strongly recommended.

minimum-wage.name: Minimum Wage Lookup
minimum-wage.description: Look up current federal and state minimum wage rates, including tipped minimum wage. Compare your state's rate to the federal minimum.
minimum-wage.shortDescription: Look up minimum wage by state.

faqs.question: Which rate applies — federal or state?
faqs.answer: Employers must pay the higher of the two. If your state rate is higher, that applies.

faqs.question: What about tipped employees?
faqs.answer: Some states allow a lower minimum for tipped workers. Tips plus wages must equal at least the standard minimum.

faqs.question: Are these rates current?
faqs.answer: We update regularly, but rates change. Verify with your state labor department.

employment-checklist.name: Employment Contract Checklist
employment-checklist.description: A comprehensive interactive checklist of everything to look for in an employment contract — compensation, benefits, restrictive covenants, termination terms, and more.

faqs.question: How many items are on the checklist?
faqs.answer: Over 30 items across 6 categories covering all major aspects of employment contracts.

faqs.question: Can I save my progress?
faqs.answer: Progress resets when you leave. Use it alongside your contract review.

faqs.question: Is this for employees or employers?
faqs.answer: Primarily designed for employees reviewing a contract before signing.

cease-desist.name: Cease and Desist Letter Generator
cease-desist.description: Generate a formal cease and desist letter for harassment, defamation, IP infringement, contract breach, or debt collection issues.
cease-desist.shortDescription: Generate C&D letters for common scenarios.

faqs.question: Is a C&D letter legally binding?
faqs.answer: A C&D letter itself is not a court order, but it creates a record and puts the recipient on formal notice.

faqs.question: Should I send it by mail or email?
faqs.answer: Certified mail with return receipt provides proof of delivery, which is important for legal proceedings.

faqs.question: What if they ignore it?
faqs.answer: If ignored, you may need to escalate to filing a lawsuit or seeking a court order.

poa-generator.name: Power of Attorney Generator
poa-generator.description: Generate a basic Power of Attorney document. Choose between general, limited, durable, or financial POA with customizable terms and notarization block.

faqs.question: What's the difference between POA types?
faqs.answer: General covers everything, Limited is for specific tasks, Durable survives incapacity, Financial covers money matters.

faqs.question: Does it need to be notarized?
faqs.answer: Most states require notarization. The template includes a notarization block.

faqs.question: Can I revoke a POA?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can revoke a POA at any time by providing written notice to the agent and any third parties.

ic-agreement.name: Independent Contractor Agreement Generator
ic-agreement.description: Generate a comprehensive independent contractor agreement covering scope of work, compensation, IP rights, confidentiality, and termination terms.
ic-agreement.shortDescription: Generate an IC agreement from a form.

faqs.question: Why is this important?
faqs.answer: A proper IC agreement helps establish the contractor relationship and avoid misclassification issues.

faqs.question: What's included?
faqs.answer: Services scope, compensation, IP assignment, confidentiality, non-solicitation, indemnification, and termination.

faqs.question: Is this for 1099 contractors?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is designed for independent contractor (1099) relationships, not employees (W-2).

promissory-note.name: Promissory Note Generator
promissory-note.description: Generate a basic promissory note with customizable terms including principal amount, interest rate, payment schedule, and default provisions.

faqs.question: Is this note legally binding?
faqs.answer: This generates a template. Have it reviewed by a lawyer and consider notarization for enforceability.

faqs.question: What interest rate should I use?
faqs.answer: Rates vary. Check your state's usury laws for maximum allowable rates. Common rates are 3-10%.

faqs.question: Does it need to be notarized?
faqs.answer: While not always required, notarization adds an extra layer of authenticity.

security-deposit.name: Security Deposit Calculator
security-deposit.description: Look up security deposit rules by state including maximum deposit amounts, interest requirements, and return deadlines after move-out.
security-deposit.shortDescription: Check security deposit rules by state.

faqs.question: Do all states limit deposits?
faqs.answer: No. Some states like Texas have no statutory limit on security deposit amounts.

faqs.question: Does my landlord owe me interest?
faqs.answer: Some states require landlords to pay interest on deposits. Check your state's rules.

faqs.question: How long to get my deposit back?
faqs.answer: Return deadlines range from 14 to 45 days depending on the state.

rent-increase.name: Rent Increase Calculator
rent-increase.description: Calculate your rent increase percentage and annual impact. Check against rent control caps in major jurisdictions.
rent-increase.shortDescription: Check if a rent increase is legal.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on rent increases?
faqs.answer: Most US jurisdictions have no limit, but some cities and states have rent control laws.

faqs.question: What's a typical annual increase?
faqs.answer: Market averages are 3-5% annually, but this varies by location.

faqs.question: Can my landlord raise rent mid-lease?
faqs.answer: Generally no, unless your lease specifically allows it. Increases typically apply at renewal.

lease-comparison.name: Lease Term Comparison
lease-comparison.description: Compare two lease offers side by side including rent, deposit, utilities, and total cost. See which lease gives you the best value.

faqs.question: What factors should I compare?
faqs.answer: Consider total cost including rent, utilities, parking, deposits, and any move-in fees.

faqs.question: Does cheaper always mean better?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Consider location, amenities, lease flexibility, and landlord reputation.

faqs.question: What about hidden costs?
faqs.answer: Factor in utilities, pet fees, parking, laundry, and any required insurance.

moveout-checklist.name: Move-Out Checklist Generator
moveout-checklist.description: Generate a customized move-out checklist based on your property type and features. Track progress with an interactive checklist.

faqs.question: Why do I need a move-out checklist?
faqs.answer: A thorough move-out increases your chance of getting your full security deposit back.

faqs.question: Should I take photos?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Document the condition of every room before and after cleaning.

faqs.question: How much notice do I need to give?
faqs.answer: Check your lease. Most require 30-60 days written notice.

rental-agreement.name: Rental Agreement Generator
rental-agreement.description: Generate a basic residential lease agreement by filling in landlord, tenant, property, and financial details. Includes standard legal provisions.

faqs.question: Is this lease legally binding?
faqs.answer: This generates a template. Have it reviewed by a real estate attorney before using.

faqs.question: Does it comply with my state's laws?
faqs.answer: It includes common provisions but state-specific requirements vary. Consult local regulations.

faqs.question: Can I customize the template?
faqs.answer: Yes, copy the generated text and modify any terms as needed.

business-name.name: Business Name Checker
business-name.description: Check your proposed business name for length, special characters, entity suffix, uniqueness, and domain potential. Includes trademark search tips.

faqs.question: Does this check if the name is taken?
faqs.answer: It provides analysis and tips. Check your state's business registry and USPTO for availability.

faqs.question: Do I need an entity suffix?
faqs.answer: Most states require an entity suffix (LLC, Inc, etc.) in your official business name.

faqs.question: How do I trademark a business name?
faqs.answer: Search the USPTO TESS database first, then file a trademark application.

contractor-vs-employee.name: Contractor vs. Employee Checker
contractor-vs-employee.description: Answer 10 questions based on IRS classification factors to determine if a worker is likely an employee or independent contractor.
contractor-vs-employee.shortDescription: Classify worker as employee or contractor.

faqs.question: Why does classification matter?
faqs.answer: Misclassification can result in back taxes, penalties, and liability for benefits.

faqs.question: What factors does the IRS consider?
faqs.answer: Behavioral control, financial control, and the type of relationship between the parties.

faqs.question: Is this definitive?
faqs.answer: No. This is a guideline. The IRS and courts consider the totality of circumstances.

severance-pay.name: Severance Pay Calculator
severance-pay.description: Estimate typical severance pay based on salary, years of service, and industry level. See low and high estimates for negotiation.

faqs.question: Is severance legally required?
faqs.answer: Generally no in the US, unless required by contract or company policy.

faqs.question: What's typical severance?
faqs.answer: Common range is 1-2 weeks of pay per year of service for standard employees.

faqs.question: Can I negotiate severance?
faqs.answer: Yes. Severance is almost always negotiable, especially for senior positions.

overtime-calc.name: Overtime Calculator
overtime-calc.description: Calculate overtime pay based on hourly rate, regular hours, overtime hours, and multiplier. See detailed pay breakdown and effective rate.

faqs.question: What's the standard overtime rate?
faqs.answer: Federal law requires 1.5x the regular rate for hours over 40 per week.

faqs.question: Are all workers eligible for overtime?
faqs.answer: No. Salaried workers meeting certain criteria may be exempt under FLSA.

faqs.question: Does my state have different rules?
faqs.answer: Some states have stricter overtime rules. California requires daily overtime after 8 hours.

partnership-split.name: Partnership Split Calculator
partnership-split.description: Calculate suggested equity and profit splits for partnerships based on capital contributions, time commitments, and customizable weighting.

faqs.question: How should we split equity?
faqs.answer: Consider capital contributions, time commitment, expertise, and risk tolerance.

faqs.question: Should profit split match equity?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Some partners may receive salary plus a smaller profit share.

faqs.question: Do we need a partnership agreement?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Always formalize splits in a written partnership agreement.

business-expense.name: Business Expense Tracker Template
business-expense.description: Generate a formatted expense tracking template customized for your business type. Includes relevant expense categories and IRS mileage rates.

faqs.question: What business types are covered?
faqs.answer: Freelance, retail, restaurant, professional services, and construction/trades.

faqs.question: Can I customize the categories?
faqs.answer: Yes, select specific categories or use the full template for your business type.

faqs.question: What records should I keep?
faqs.answer: Keep receipts for all expenses over $75 and maintain a mileage log for vehicle deductions.

crypto-tax.name: Crypto Tax Calculator
crypto-tax.description: Calculate capital gains or losses on cryptocurrency trades. Estimates short-term vs long-term tax based on your holding period and tax bracket.

faqs.question: How is crypto taxed?
faqs.answer: In the US, crypto is taxed as property. Short-term gains (held < 1 year) are taxed as ordinary income; long-term gains get preferential rates.

faqs.question: What's the long-term capital gains rate?
faqs.answer: Most taxpayers pay 15% on long-term gains. Higher earners may pay 20%.

faqs.question: Do I owe tax on losses?
faqs.answer: No, but you can use losses to offset gains and deduct up to $3,000 against ordinary income.

dca-calculator.name: DCA Calculator
dca-calculator.description: Simulate a dollar-cost averaging strategy. See your average cost basis, total units acquired, and return based on regular periodic investments.

faqs.question: What is dollar-cost averaging?
faqs.answer: DCA means investing a fixed amount at regular intervals regardless of price, reducing the impact of volatility.

faqs.question: Is DCA better than lump sum?
faqs.answer: Historically, lump sum outperforms about 2/3 of the time, but DCA reduces risk and emotional decision-making.

faqs.question: Does this use real prices?
faqs.answer: It simulates a linear price path between your start and end prices for estimation purposes.

position-size.name: Position Size Calculator
position-size.description: Calculate the optimal position size based on your account size, risk tolerance, and stop loss distance. Essential for proper risk management.

faqs.question: How much should I risk per trade?
faqs.answer: Most professional traders risk 1-2% of their account per trade to survive losing streaks.

faqs.question: What is a stop loss?
faqs.answer: A stop loss is a predetermined price at which you exit a losing trade to limit your loss.

faqs.question: Does this work for crypto?
faqs.answer: Yes, position sizing applies to stocks, crypto, forex, and any tradeable asset.

profit-loss.name: Profit & Loss Calculator
profit-loss.description: Calculate your profit or loss on a trade including leverage and trading fees. See gross P&L, net P&L, and percentage return.

faqs.question: How does leverage affect P&L?
faqs.answer: Leverage multiplies both gains and losses. 10× leverage means a 1% move results in a 10% gain or loss.

faqs.question: What are typical trading fees?
faqs.answer: Major exchanges charge 0.04-0.1% for makers and 0.06-0.2% for takers per trade.

faqs.question: Is the fee applied per side?
faqs.answer: Yes, you pay fees both when opening and closing a position (round-trip).

compound-interest.name: Compound Interest Calculator
compound-interest.description: Project the growth of your investment with compound interest. Factor in initial principal, interest rate, compounding frequency, and monthly contributions.

faqs.question: What is compound interest?
faqs.answer: Compound interest is interest earned on both the initial principal and previously accumulated interest — 'interest on interest.'

faqs.question: How often should interest compound?
faqs.answer: More frequent compounding yields higher returns. Daily compounding earns slightly more than monthly or quarterly.

faqs.question: Is this the same as APY?
faqs.answer: APY (Annual Percentage Yield) already accounts for compounding. The rate you enter here is the nominal annual rate.

margin-call.name: Margin Call Calculator
margin-call.description: Determine at what price your broker will issue a margin call. Input your equity, borrowed amount, and maintenance margin requirement.
margin-call.shortDescription: Calculate your margin call trigger price.

faqs.question: What is a margin call?
faqs.answer: A margin call occurs when your account equity falls below the maintenance margin requirement, forcing you to deposit more funds or sell assets.

faqs.question: What is the typical maintenance margin?
faqs.answer: FINRA requires a minimum 25% maintenance margin, but most brokers require 30-40%.

faqs.question: What happens if I can't meet a margin call?
faqs.answer: Your broker may liquidate your positions at market price to bring your account back to the required margin level.

breakeven-calc.name: Trade Breakeven Calculator
breakeven-calc.description: Calculate the exact breakeven price for a trade accounting for round-trip trading fees and leverage. Know the minimum move needed to profit.
breakeven-calc.shortDescription: Find your breakeven price after fees.

faqs.question: Why is breakeven different from entry price?
faqs.answer: Trading fees mean you need the price to move slightly in your favor just to break even.

faqs.question: How do fees scale with leverage?
faqs.answer: Fees are typically charged on the full leveraged position size, so higher leverage means higher absolute fee costs.

faqs.question: Should I factor in spread?
faqs.answer: Yes, the bid-ask spread is an additional cost. This calculator focuses on exchange fees.

risk-reward.name: Risk/Reward Calculator
risk-reward.description: Analyze the risk-to-reward ratio of a trade. Input your entry, stop loss, and take profit to see if the trade is worth taking.

faqs.question: What is a good risk/reward ratio?
faqs.answer: Most traders aim for at least 1:2 (risking $1 to make $2). Higher ratios allow profitability with lower win rates.

faqs.question: What does the required win rate mean?
faqs.answer: It's the minimum percentage of trades you need to win to break even at this R:R ratio.

faqs.question: Should every trade have a good R:R?
faqs.answer: Ideally yes, but some strategies use tight R:R with high win rates. Consistency matters more than any single trade.

crypto-converter.name: Crypto Converter
crypto-converter.description: Convert between major cryptocurrencies using reference prices. See equivalent values across BTC, ETH, SOL, and other popular assets.

faqs.question: Are these real-time prices?
faqs.answer: No, this tool uses static reference prices for demonstration. Use a live exchange for actual trading prices.

faqs.question: Which cryptocurrencies are supported?
faqs.answer: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, ADA, DOGE, and DOT are currently supported.

faqs.question: Can I use this for tax calculations?
faqs.answer: For tax purposes, use the actual transaction prices from your exchange records.

loan-payment.name: Loan Payment Calculator
loan-payment.description: Calculate monthly loan payments using standard amortization. See your monthly payment, total interest paid, and total cost of the loan.

faqs.question: How is the monthly payment calculated?
faqs.answer: Using the standard amortization formula: M = P[r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n - 1], where P is principal, r is monthly rate, n is number of payments.

faqs.question: Does this include taxes and insurance?
faqs.answer: No, this calculates principal and interest only. Your total housing payment may include taxes, insurance, and PMI.

faqs.question: Can I use this for auto loans?
faqs.answer: Yes, it works for any fixed-rate amortizing loan — mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, etc.

grant-budget.name: Grant Budget Calculator
grant-budget.description: Allocate grant funds across budget categories like personnel, equipment, travel, and indirect costs. Track remaining funds and get warnings when allocations exceed the total grant amount.
grant-budget.shortDescription: Allocate and track grant budget spending.

faqs.question: What is an indirect cost rate?
faqs.answer: An indirect cost rate covers overhead expenses (facilities, administration) not directly tied to the grant project. It's applied as a percentage of direct costs.

faqs.question: What are common budget categories?
faqs.answer: Personnel, equipment, travel, supplies, contractual services, and other direct costs are standard categories for most federal and state grants.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed the budget?
faqs.answer: The calculator will warn you. In practice, exceeding approved budgets may require a formal budget modification request to the grantor.

grant-deadline.name: Grant Deadline Tracker
grant-deadline.description: Track grant application deadlines, reporting due dates, and milestones. See countdown timers, urgency badges, and a sorted timeline of all upcoming obligations.
grant-deadline.shortDescription: Track grant deadlines and reporting dates.

faqs.question: What types of deadlines should I track?
faqs.answer: Application deadlines, progress reports, financial reports, final reports, and any interim milestones required by the grantor.

faqs.question: What happens if I miss a reporting deadline?
faqs.answer: Missing deadlines can result in funding suspension, required corrective action plans, or disqualification from future grants.

faqs.question: Is this data saved?
faqs.answer: Currently the tracker is session-based. Refresh will clear entries. Create an account to save analyses for future reference.

grant-compliance.name: Grant Compliance Checklist
grant-compliance.description: Generate a compliance checklist based on your grant type — federal, state, foundation, or corporate. Covers reporting, audits, procurement, matching funds, and record-keeping requirements.

faqs.question: What is 2 CFR 200?
faqs.answer: The Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200) sets administrative, cost, and audit requirements for federal awards to non-federal entities.

faqs.question: When is a single audit required?
faqs.answer: Organizations spending $750,000 or more in federal awards in a fiscal year must have a single audit performed.

faqs.question: Do foundation grants have compliance requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, though typically less complex than federal grants. Common requirements include narrative reports, financial summaries, and use-of-funds restrictions.

vesting-schedule.name: Vesting Schedule Calculator
vesting-schedule.description: Calculate your equity vesting timeline based on total shares, vesting period, cliff, and frequency. See a detailed table of vested shares per period with cumulative totals.

faqs.question: What is a cliff period?
faqs.answer: A cliff is the initial period (usually 12 months) before any shares vest. After the cliff, a lump sum vests and the remainder vests on a regular schedule.

faqs.question: What is a typical vesting schedule?
faqs.answer: The most common is a 4-year schedule with a 1-year cliff and monthly vesting thereafter (known as '4-year with 1-year cliff').

faqs.question: What happens if I leave before the cliff?
faqs.answer: You forfeit all unvested shares. If you leave before the cliff, you receive no shares at all.

stock-option-tax.name: Stock Option Tax Calculator
stock-option-tax.description: Estimate taxes on stock option exercises for ISOs and NSOs. Calculates ordinary income tax, capital gains tax, and AMT exposure based on your holding period and tax bracket.
stock-option-tax.shortDescription: Estimate taxes on stock option exercises.

faqs.question: What is the difference between ISO and NSO?
faqs.answer: ISOs (Incentive Stock Options) offer potential tax advantages if holding period requirements are met. NSOs (Non-Qualified Stock Options) are taxed as ordinary income at exercise.

faqs.question: What is AMT?
faqs.answer: Alternative Minimum Tax is a parallel tax system. For ISOs, the bargain element at exercise is an AMT preference item that may trigger additional tax liability.

faqs.question: What is a qualifying disposition for ISOs?
faqs.answer: You must hold the shares for at least 1 year after exercise AND 2 years after the grant date to qualify for long-term capital gains treatment.

equity-dilution.name: Equity Dilution Calculator
equity-dilution.description: Calculate how funding rounds and option pool expansions affect your ownership percentage. See pre- and post-dilution ownership side by side with the total dilution impact.
equity-dilution.shortDescription: Calculate equity dilution from funding rounds.

faqs.question: What is equity dilution?
faqs.answer: Dilution occurs when new shares are issued (via funding rounds or option pools), reducing existing shareholders' percentage ownership even though their share count stays the same.

faqs.question: Is dilution always bad?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. If the new investment increases the company's valuation significantly, your smaller percentage may be worth more in absolute dollar terms.

faqs.question: What is an option pool?
faqs.answer: A reserve of shares set aside for future employee equity grants. Investors often require an option pool be created or expanded before funding.

solar-roi.name: Solar Panel ROI Calculator
solar-roi.description: Calculate the return on investment for solar panel installation. Input system cost, energy production, electricity rate, and incentives to see payback period, 25-year savings, and ROI percentage.
solar-roi.shortDescription: Calculate solar panel payback period and ROI.

faqs.question: How long does it take for solar panels to pay for themselves?
faqs.answer: Typically 6–10 years depending on system cost, local electricity rates, and available incentives. After payback, the electricity generated is essentially free.

faqs.question: What affects solar ROI the most?
faqs.answer: The biggest factors are system cost, local electricity rates, available incentives/rebates, and how much sun your location receives annually.

faqs.question: Do solar panels increase home value?
faqs.answer: Studies show solar panels can increase home value by 3-4% on average, providing additional ROI beyond energy savings.

solar-incentive.name: Solar Incentive & Tax Credit Estimator
solar-incentive.description: Estimate federal and state solar incentives including the 30% Investment Tax Credit (ITC), state rebates, and SREC values. See your net cost after all available incentives.
solar-incentive.shortDescription: Estimate solar tax credits and state rebates.

faqs.question: What is the federal solar ITC?
faqs.answer: The Investment Tax Credit allows you to deduct 30% of the cost of installing a solar energy system from your federal taxes. This rate is available through 2032.

faqs.question: What are SRECs?
faqs.answer: Solar Renewable Energy Certificates represent the environmental benefits of solar electricity. In some states, you can sell SRECs for additional income.

faqs.question: Can I combine federal and state incentives?
faqs.answer: Yes, the federal ITC can be combined with state rebates and SRECs. However, some state incentives may reduce the cost basis for the federal credit.

energy-savings.name: Energy Savings Calculator
energy-savings.description: Calculate how much you can save on electricity by going solar. Input your current bill, solar coverage percentage, and system cost to see monthly, annual, and lifetime savings.
energy-savings.shortDescription: Calculate electricity savings from solar power.

faqs.question: What solar coverage percentage should I aim for?
faqs.answer: Most homeowners aim for 80-100% coverage. Going above 100% may not be cost-effective unless your utility offers favorable net metering.

faqs.question: Do solar savings increase over time?
faqs.answer: Yes, as utility rates typically increase 3-4% annually, your savings grow each year while your solar production costs remain fixed.

faqs.question: What's included in the break-even calculation?
faqs.answer: Break-even accounts for total system cost minus the monthly savings until cumulative savings equal the initial investment.

carbon-footprint.name: Carbon Footprint Offset Calculator
carbon-footprint.description: Calculate the CO₂ emissions you can avoid by switching to renewable energy. See equivalents in trees planted and driving miles offset based on your energy source and usage.
carbon-footprint.shortDescription: Calculate CO₂ savings from switching to renewables.

faqs.question: How much CO₂ does the average US home produce?
faqs.answer: The average US home produces about 7.5 metric tons of CO₂ per year from electricity use, though this varies significantly by energy source.

faqs.question: How are trees-equivalent calculated?
faqs.answer: A mature tree absorbs approximately 22 kg (0.022 metric tons) of CO₂ per year. We divide your avoided emissions by this factor.

faqs.question: What energy source has the highest carbon footprint?
faqs.answer: Coal has the highest emissions at about 0.95 kg CO₂/kWh, followed by oil (0.72) and natural gas (0.42). The US grid average is about 0.39 kg CO₂/kWh.

green-lease.name: Green Lease Clause Checker
green-lease.description: Evaluate your commercial lease for green provisions. Check which sustainability clauses are present and get a score with recommendations for missing essential provisions.
green-lease.shortDescription: Check green clauses in commercial leases.

faqs.question: What is a green lease?
faqs.answer: A green lease includes provisions that align landlord and tenant incentives for energy efficiency, sustainability, and environmental performance in commercial buildings.

faqs.question: Why are energy benchmarking clauses important?
faqs.answer: Energy benchmarking is required by law in many cities and helps track building performance over time, identifying opportunities for efficiency improvements.

faqs.question: What is a cost-sharing clause?
faqs.answer: Cost-sharing clauses define how energy efficiency upgrade costs and resulting savings are split between landlord and tenant, solving the 'split incentive' problem.

ev-vs-gas.name: EV vs Gas Cost Comparison
ev-vs-gas.description: Compare the annual fuel costs of driving an electric vehicle versus a gas vehicle. See side-by-side costs, annual savings, and 10-year projected savings.
ev-vs-gas.shortDescription: Compare EV and gas vehicle fuel costs.

faqs.question: How much cheaper is it to drive an EV?
faqs.answer: On average, EVs cost 50-70% less to fuel than gas vehicles, depending on local electricity and gas prices.

faqs.question: What is EV efficiency measured in?
faqs.answer: EV efficiency is typically measured in kWh per 100 miles. Most modern EVs range from 25-35 kWh per 100 miles.

faqs.question: Does this include maintenance savings?
faqs.answer: This calculator focuses on fuel costs only. EVs also typically save $500-1000/year on maintenance due to fewer moving parts.

home-energy-audit.name: Home Energy Audit Checklist
home-energy-audit.description: Generate a prioritized checklist of energy improvements for your home. Select your home type and age to see applicable upgrades with estimated savings ranges.
home-energy-audit.shortDescription: Get a personalized home energy improvement checklist.

faqs.question: What's the most impactful energy improvement?
faqs.answer: For most homes, attic insulation and air sealing provide the best ROI, reducing heating/cooling costs by 10-20%.

faqs.question: Should I get a professional energy audit?
faqs.answer: Yes, a professional audit with blower door testing can identify hidden air leaks and insulation gaps that visual inspection misses.

faqs.question: Do these savings estimates include rebates?
faqs.answer: The savings shown are from reduced energy bills only. Many improvements also qualify for federal or state rebates, improving the ROI further.

ppa-calculator.name: Power Purchase Agreement Calculator
ppa-calculator.description: Compare the total cost of a Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) versus utility electricity over the contract term. See year-by-year cost breakdowns including rate escalations.
ppa-calculator.shortDescription: Compare PPA costs vs utility electricity.

faqs.question: What is a PPA?
faqs.answer: A Power Purchase Agreement is a contract where a solar provider installs panels on your property and sells you the electricity at a fixed rate, typically lower than utility rates.

faqs.question: What is a typical PPA escalation rate?
faqs.answer: PPA escalation rates typically range from 1-3% annually, compared to utility rate increases of 3-5% per year historically.

faqs.question: Who owns the panels in a PPA?
faqs.answer: The solar provider owns and maintains the panels. At the end of the contract term (usually 15-25 years), you may have the option to buy the system or have it removed.

income-tax.name: Income Tax Estimator
income-tax.description: Estimate your federal income tax based on filing status, gross income, and deductions. See a bracket-by-bracket breakdown, effective tax rate, and take-home pay.
income-tax.shortDescription: Estimate federal income tax by bracket.

faqs.question: What tax year does this use?
faqs.answer: It uses 2024 federal tax brackets and standard deduction amounts.

faqs.question: Does it include state taxes?
faqs.answer: No, this estimates federal income tax only. State taxes vary by location.

faqs.question: What if I itemize deductions?
faqs.answer: Enter your total itemized deductions in the deductions field instead of using the standard deduction.

auto-loan.name: Auto Loan Calculator
auto-loan.description: Calculate your monthly auto loan payment including down payment and trade-in value. See total interest paid and the true cost of your vehicle purchase.

faqs.question: What's a good auto loan interest rate?
faqs.answer: Rates vary by credit score. Excellent credit may get 4-6%, while average credit may see 7-12%.

faqs.question: Should I make a larger down payment?
faqs.answer: A larger down payment reduces your loan amount, monthly payment, and total interest paid.

faqs.question: How does trade-in value affect the loan?
faqs.answer: Trade-in value is subtracted from the vehicle price, reducing the amount you need to finance.

debt-payoff.name: Debt Payoff Calculator
debt-payoff.description: See how long it takes to pay off debt with minimum payments vs extra payments. Compare total interest paid and time saved by paying more each month.
debt-payoff.shortDescription: Calculate debt payoff time and interest savings.

faqs.question: How much extra should I pay?
faqs.answer: Even $50-100 extra per month can save thousands in interest and years of payments on high-interest debt.

faqs.question: Should I pay off high-interest debt first?
faqs.answer: The avalanche method (highest rate first) saves the most money. The snowball method (smallest balance first) provides quicker wins.

faqs.question: Does this work for credit cards?
faqs.answer: Yes, it works for any debt with a fixed interest rate and regular payments.

net-worth.name: Net Worth Calculator
net-worth.description: Calculate your net worth by listing your assets and liabilities. Track cash, investments, property, and debts to see your overall financial position.

faqs.question: What counts as an asset?
faqs.answer: Cash, savings, investments, retirement accounts, real estate, vehicles, and any other property with value.

faqs.question: How often should I calculate net worth?
faqs.answer: Monthly or quarterly tracking helps you see financial progress and identify trends.

faqs.question: Is negative net worth bad?
faqs.answer: It's common early in life (student loans, mortgage). Focus on the trend — it should improve over time.

paycheck-calc.name: Paycheck Calculator
paycheck-calc.description: Estimate your take-home pay per paycheck. Enter salary or hourly rate, filing status, and pay frequency to see federal tax, Social Security, and Medicare withholdings.

faqs.question: Does this include state taxes?
faqs.answer: No, this estimates federal withholdings only. Your actual paycheck may also have state and local taxes deducted.

faqs.question: What about pre-tax deductions?
faqs.answer: This doesn't account for 401(k), health insurance, or other pre-tax deductions which would reduce your taxable income.

faqs.question: Is bi-weekly the same as semi-monthly?
faqs.answer: No. Bi-weekly means every two weeks (26 paychecks/year). Semi-monthly means twice a month (24 paychecks/year).

settlement-estimator.name: Settlement Value Estimator
settlement-estimator.description: Estimate your personal injury or auto accident settlement value. Enter medical bills, lost wages, and injury severity to get a range based on the multiplier method.

faqs.question: How accurate is the settlement estimator?
faqs.answer: It provides a rough range using the multiplier method commonly used by insurance companies. Actual settlements depend on many factors including fault, jurisdiction, and insurer.

faqs.question: What is the multiplier method?
faqs.answer: Economic damages (medical bills, lost wages) are multiplied by a factor of 1.5 to 10 based on injury severity to estimate total compensation.

faqs.question: Should I rely on this for my case?
faqs.answer: No. Use this as a starting point to understand potential value. Consult a personal injury lawyer for an accurate case evaluation.

insurance-quote-comparison.name: Insurance Quote Comparison Tool
insurance-quote-comparison.description: Compare auto, home, and life insurance quotes side by side. Enter quotes from multiple companies and see which offers the best value.
insurance-quote-comparison.shortDescription: Compare insurance quotes side by side.

faqs.question: How many quotes should I compare?
faqs.answer: At least 3-5 quotes from different companies. Rates can vary by hundreds of dollars for the same coverage.

faqs.question: What is effective cost?
faqs.answer: Effective cost combines your annual premium with your deductible, giving a more complete picture of total potential cost.

faqs.question: Does this tool provide actual quotes?
faqs.answer: No. You enter quotes you've received from insurance companies. This tool helps you organize and compare them.

accident-damage.name: Accident Damage Calculator
accident-damage.description: Calculate the total damages from an accident including medical bills, property damage, lost wages, and pain and suffering. See a complete breakdown.

faqs.question: What are economic vs. non-economic damages?
faqs.answer: Economic damages are quantifiable losses (medical bills, lost wages, property damage). Non-economic damages are subjective (pain, suffering, emotional distress).

faqs.question: How do I estimate pain and suffering?
faqs.answer: A common method is 1.5-5x your medical bills, depending on injury severity. More severe injuries warrant higher multipliers.

faqs.question: Does this include punitive damages?
faqs.answer: No. Punitive damages are rare and only awarded to punish extreme misconduct. This calculator focuses on compensatory damages.

attorney-fee.name: Attorney Fee Calculator
attorney-fee.description: Calculate attorney fees for contingency and hourly arrangements. See how much your lawyer will earn and what your take-home settlement will be.

faqs.question: What is a typical contingency fee?
faqs.answer: Most personal injury lawyers charge 33% for pre-litigation settlements and 40% if the case goes to trial.

faqs.question: Are case expenses separate from the fee?
faqs.answer: Usually yes. Filing fees, expert witnesses, and medical record costs are typically deducted separately from your settlement.

faqs.question: Can I negotiate the fee percentage?
faqs.answer: Yes, contingency fees are negotiable. High-value cases with clear liability may warrant a lower percentage.

insurance-premium.name: Insurance Premium Estimator
insurance-premium.description: Estimate monthly insurance premiums for auto, home, renters, and life insurance based on your age, credit, location, and coverage level.

faqs.question: How accurate is this estimate?
faqs.answer: This provides a rough estimate based on national averages and common rate factors. Actual premiums depend on your specific profile and insurer.

faqs.question: Does credit score really affect insurance?
faqs.answer: In most states, yes. Insurers use credit-based insurance scores to set rates. Better credit typically means lower premiums.

faqs.question: What's the biggest factor in insurance cost?
faqs.answer: For auto: driving record and age. For home: location and property value. For life: age and health status.

force-majeure-checker.name: Force Majeure Clause Checker
force-majeure-checker.description: Paste your contract and instantly see whether the force majeure clause is strong, narrow, or missing. Detects covered events, catch-all language, notice requirements, and duty-to-mitigate clauses.
force-majeure-checker.shortDescription: Detect and rate force majeure clauses.

faqs.question: What is force majeure?
faqs.answer: A clause excusing performance when extraordinary events (war, pandemic, natural disasters) make it impossible.

faqs.question: Why does strength matter?
faqs.answer: Narrow clauses may not cover the actual event, leaving you in breach.

faqs.question: Should I add a catch-all?
faqs.answer: Catch-all language ('any event beyond reasonable control') broadens protection significantly.

auto-renewal-detector.name: Auto-Renewal Clause Detector
auto-renewal-detector.description: Find evergreen and auto-renewal language in any contract. Surfaces the notice window so you don't miss a cancellation deadline.

faqs.question: Why are auto-renewals risky?
faqs.answer: Many require 30–90 days advance written notice or you're locked in another year.

faqs.question: Are auto-renewal clauses legal?
faqs.answer: Yes, but several states require clear conspicuous disclosure for consumer contracts.

faqs.question: What should I do if I find one?
faqs.answer: Set a calendar reminder for the notice deadline immediately.

governing-law-id.name: Governing Law Identifier
governing-law-id.description: Extract governing law, venue, arbitration, jury-trial waivers and class-action waivers from any contract in seconds.

faqs.question: Why does governing law matter?
faqs.answer: It determines which state's laws apply and where you'd have to sue.

faqs.question: What's a jury waiver?
faqs.answer: A clause forcing disputes to be decided by a judge instead of a jury.

faqs.question: Can I negotiate venue?
faqs.answer: Often yes, especially with vendors. Inconvenient venues can dramatically increase litigation costs.

plain-english-rewriter.name: Plain English Rewriter
plain-english-rewriter.description: Convert legalese into plain English. Replaces archaic legal phrases (heretofore, pursuant to, notwithstanding) with clear modern equivalents.

faqs.question: Is the rewrite legally accurate?
faqs.answer: It preserves meaning by swapping equivalent terms. Always verify with a lawyer for critical contracts.

faqs.question: What gets rewritten?
faqs.answer: Common archaic phrases — 'shall' becomes 'must', 'pursuant to' becomes 'under', etc.

faqs.question: Does it explain legal terms?
faqs.answer: Yes, it pulls definitions from our legal dictionary for any specialized terms found.

chargeback-checker.name: Chargeback Eligibility Checker
chargeback-checker.description: Answer a few questions to see whether you're likely eligible for a credit-card chargeback under the Fair Credit Billing Act.
chargeback-checker.shortDescription: Check if you can win a chargeback.

faqs.question: How long do I have to dispute?
faqs.answer: 60 days from the statement date under federal law; many issuers allow 120 days.

faqs.question: What's the difference vs. a refund?
faqs.answer: A chargeback is filed with your bank, not the merchant. The bank reverses the charge while it investigates.

faqs.question: Can the merchant fight it?
faqs.answer: Yes. Strong documentation greatly improves your odds.

lemon-law-qualifier.name: Lemon Law Qualifier
lemon-law-qualifier.description: Check whether your defective vehicle likely qualifies under your state's lemon law based on repair attempts, days out of service, age, and mileage.
lemon-law-qualifier.shortDescription: Check if your vehicle qualifies as a lemon.

faqs.question: What is a 'lemon'?
faqs.answer: A vehicle with a substantial defect that can't be fixed after a reasonable number of attempts.

faqs.question: Common thresholds?
faqs.answer: Typically 3–4 repair attempts for the same issue or 30+ days out of service.

faqs.question: Do used cars qualify?
faqs.answer: Some states extend lemon-law protection to used cars; many do not.

subscription-cancel-letter.name: Subscription Cancellation Letter
subscription-cancel-letter.description: Generate a written cancellation letter for any subscription (gym, SaaS, magazine) that requires written notice.

faqs.question: Why send a written letter?
faqs.answer: Many subscription contracts only honor written cancellations and ignore phone requests.

faqs.question: Should I send by certified mail?
faqs.answer: For high-value or contested cancellations, yes — for the proof of delivery.

faqs.question: Does this stop charges immediately?
faqs.answer: Per the letter wording, you're requesting immediate cancellation, but check your contract for required notice windows.

data-breach-checklist.name: Data Breach Response Checklist
data-breach-checklist.description: Step-by-step checklist for what to do after receiving a data-breach notification — from changing passwords to filing IRS Form 14039.
data-breach-checklist.shortDescription: Steps to take after a data breach.

faqs.question: Should I freeze my credit?
faqs.answer: Yes — credit freezes are free in all 50 states and far more protective than fraud alerts.

faqs.question: Is the free monitoring enough?
faqs.answer: It's helpful but doesn't prevent fraud. Combine it with a freeze.

faqs.question: Do I need to do anything if SSN wasn't exposed?
faqs.answer: Still change passwords and enable 2FA. Email + password breaches enable account takeovers.

final-paycheck-lookup.name: Final Paycheck Deadline Lookup
final-paycheck-lookup.description: When is your last paycheck legally due? Lookup by state for both termination and resignation scenarios.
final-paycheck-lookup.shortDescription: When your final paycheck is legally due.

faqs.question: What if my employer is late?
faqs.answer: Many states impose waiting-time penalties — sometimes a full day's wages per day late.

faqs.question: Does this include unused PTO?
faqs.answer: In most states, accrued vacation must be paid out. Sick leave often doesn't have to be.

faqs.question: What about commissions?
faqs.answer: Earned commissions are wages and must be paid in the final check.

unemployment-estimator.name: Unemployment Benefits Estimator
unemployment-estimator.description: Estimate your weekly unemployment benefit based on your prior wages and state cap.

faqs.question: Who qualifies?
faqs.answer: Generally workers laid off through no fault of their own who meet a state's wage and work-history requirements.

faqs.question: Is the estimate exact?
faqs.answer: No — actual benefits depend on quarterly earnings during a state-defined base period.

faqs.question: How long do benefits last?
faqs.answer: Up to 26 weeks in most states; some states are shorter or extended during recessions.

nda-vs-noncompete.name: NDA vs. Non-Compete Comparator
nda-vs-noncompete.description: Side-by-side comparison of NDAs and non-competes — purpose, scope, duration, enforceability, and red flags.
nda-vs-noncompete.shortDescription: Compare NDAs and non-competes side by side.

faqs.question: Can I be required to sign both?
faqs.answer: Yes. They serve different purposes and many employers use both.

faqs.question: Are non-competes always enforceable?
faqs.answer: No. California, North Dakota, Oklahoma, and Minnesota largely ban them. The FTC has proposed a national ban.

faqs.question: What makes an NDA fair?
faqs.answer: Reasonable definition of confidential info, reasonable duration, and clear exclusions for public knowledge.

discrimination-checklist.name: Workplace Discrimination Claim Checklist
discrimination-checklist.description: Prepare to file an EEOC charge — documentation, comparator analysis, internal reporting, and retaliation tracking.

faqs.question: What's the EEOC deadline?
faqs.answer: 180 days from the discriminatory act (300 days in states with their own anti-discrimination agency).

faqs.question: Do I need a lawyer?
faqs.answer: Not to file with the EEOC, but strongly recommended before signing any settlement or release.

faqs.question: Is retaliation illegal?
faqs.answer: Yes — retaliating against someone for filing or supporting a discrimination complaint is itself unlawful.

eviction-notice-lookup.name: Eviction Notice Period Lookup
eviction-notice-lookup.description: Look up the legally required eviction notice period by state and reason — non-payment, lease violation, or no-fault termination.

faqs.question: Can a landlord skip the notice?
faqs.answer: No. Bypassing the notice and filing immediately is grounds for dismissal.

faqs.question: Do local rules differ?
faqs.answer: Yes. Cities with rent control often require longer notices and 'just cause'.

faqs.question: What if the tenant cures?
faqs.answer: If the notice is a 'cure or quit' and the tenant cures (pays rent or fixes the violation), eviction usually cannot proceed.

habitability-tracker.name: Habitability Repair Request Generator
habitability-tracker.description: Generate a written repair request invoking the implied warranty of habitability — the foundation for rent withholding or repair-and-deduct remedies.
habitability-tracker.shortDescription: Generate a written habitability repair request.

faqs.question: What is the implied warranty of habitability?
faqs.answer: A common-law (and in many states statutory) requirement that rentals be safe and livable.

faqs.question: Can I withhold rent?
faqs.answer: Sometimes — but only after written notice and a reasonable cure period, and the procedure varies by state.

faqs.question: What counts as a habitability issue?
faqs.answer: No heat, no hot water, no running water, structural defects, vermin, mold, or unsafe wiring.

mortgage-affordability.name: Mortgage Affordability Calculator
mortgage-affordability.description: Calculate your maximum home price using the 36% debt-to-income rule, accounting for taxes, insurance, and existing debts.
mortgage-affordability.shortDescription: Calculate your max affordable home price.

faqs.question: What is DTI?
faqs.answer: Debt-to-income — the percentage of your monthly gross income that goes to debt payments.

faqs.question: Why 36%?
faqs.answer: Conventional underwriting limits total monthly debt (including the new mortgage) to about 36% of gross income.

faqs.question: Does this include PMI?
faqs.answer: PMI applies if you put less than 20% down and adds roughly 0.5–1% of the loan annually. Not separately calculated here.

operating-agreement-llc.name: LLC Operating Agreement Generator
operating-agreement-llc.description: Generate a basic LLC operating agreement covering members, capital, profits, management, and dissolution.
operating-agreement-llc.shortDescription: Generate a basic LLC operating agreement.

faqs.question: Do I need an operating agreement?
faqs.answer: Most states don't require one but strongly recommend it — and a few (CA, DE, NY, MO, ME) do require one.

faqs.question: Can I customize it later?
faqs.answer: Yes. This is a starting template. Amend as your business grows.

faqs.question: Is this a substitute for a lawyer?
faqs.answer: No. For multi-member LLCs or significant capital, have an attorney review.

founders-agreement.name: Founders Agreement Generator
founders-agreement.description: Draft a founders agreement covering equity splits, vesting (1-year cliff, 4-year), IP assignment, roles, and departure.
founders-agreement.shortDescription: Draft a founders agreement with vesting & IP.

faqs.question: Why vesting for founders?
faqs.answer: Protects the team if a co-founder leaves early. Without vesting, they keep their full equity.

faqs.question: What's a 1-year cliff?
faqs.answer: If a founder leaves within the first year, they get zero shares. After year one, shares vest gradually.

faqs.question: Does this replace incorporation?
faqs.answer: No — it's a side agreement. You still need to file with the state to form the entity.

dmca-generator.name: DMCA Takedown Notice Generator
dmca-generator.description: Generate a DMCA takedown notice that meets the 17 U.S.C. § 512(c) requirements for removing infringing content.

faqs.question: Where do I send it?
faqs.answer: To the platform's designated DMCA agent (listed in their terms or at copyright.gov).

faqs.question: What if it's a false claim?
faqs.answer: Filing a knowingly false DMCA notice exposes you to perjury and civil liability.

faqs.question: Can the user fight back?
faqs.answer: Yes — they can file a counter-notice, and the content may be restored within 10–14 days.

emergency-fund.name: Emergency Fund Calculator
emergency-fund.description: Calculate how much you need in an emergency fund and how long it will take to reach your goal at your current savings rate.

faqs.question: How many months should I save?
faqs.answer: 3 months for dual-income households with stable jobs; 6+ months for single income or variable income.

faqs.question: Where should I keep it?
faqs.answer: A high-yield savings account — accessible but separate from checking.

faqs.question: What counts as 'expenses'?
faqs.answer: Essentials only: housing, food, utilities, insurance, minimum debt payments, transportation.

tip-splitter.name: Tip & Service Charge Splitter
tip-splitter.description: Split a restaurant bill across multiple people including tax and tip — with handling for mandatory service charges (which are not legally tips).
tip-splitter.shortDescription: Split bills with tax, tip, and service charges.

faqs.question: Is a service charge a tip?
faqs.answer: No. Service charges are restaurant revenue and may not go to staff; tips legally must.

faqs.question: Should I tip on top of a service charge?
faqs.answer: Generally no — but ask, since policies vary.

faqs.question: Do I tip on tax?
faqs.answer: Most people tip on the pre-tax subtotal. This calculator does the same.

net-metering.name: Net Metering Savings Estimator
net-metering.description: Estimate annual savings from solar net metering — production offset plus credits for excess energy exported to the grid.

faqs.question: What's net metering?
faqs.answer: A billing arrangement where you get credit for excess solar power you send to the grid.

faqs.question: Net metering vs. net billing?
faqs.answer: Net metering credits you at retail rates; net billing pays a lower wholesale rate. Policy depends on your state and utility.

faqs.question: Are there caps?
faqs.answer: Some utilities cap the annual credit value or roll it over to next year only at a discount.

child-support.name: Child Support Calculator
child-support.description: Estimate monthly child support obligations using your state's income shares or percentage-of-income guideline. Supports California, New York, Texas, Florida, Illinois, and a generic fallback.
child-support.shortDescription: Estimate monthly child support by state.

faqs.question: How accurate is this estimate?
faqs.answer: It applies the published state guideline formula to the inputs you provide. Actual orders may vary based on custody arrangements, healthcare costs, and judicial discretion.

faqs.question: Does it account for shared custody?
faqs.answer: Partially — many states adjust support based on overnights. This calculator gives a baseline; your final order may be reduced for shared parenting time.

faqs.question: Is the result legally binding?
faqs.answer: No. It is an estimate for planning purposes only. Final support orders are issued by a court.

alimony-calc.name: Alimony / Spousal Support Calculator
alimony-calc.description: Estimate monthly alimony (spousal support / maintenance) and likely duration based on incomes, length of marriage, and state guidelines.

faqs.question: How is alimony calculated?
faqs.answer: Most states use a formula based on the difference in spouse incomes plus length of marriage. Some, like California (post-judgment) and Texas, give judges wide discretion.

faqs.question: How long does alimony last?
faqs.answer: Often 30–50% of the length of the marriage. Marriages of 20+ years may receive indefinite support in some states.

faqs.question: Can the amount change later?
faqs.answer: Yes — most alimony orders can be modified if there is a substantial change in income, remarriage, or cohabitation.

divorce-cost.name: Divorce Cost Estimator
